content.problemStatement:
Chef is baking delicious cookies today! Since Chef is super hungry, he wants to eat at least NNN cookies.

Since Chef is a messy eater, he drops a lot of crumbs. Crumbs of BBB cookies can be put together to make a new cookie!

Given NNN and BBB, help Chef find out the minimum number of cookies he must initially bake, AAA, to satisfy his hunger.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of  two space separated integers N,BN, BN,B.
###Output:
For each test case, print a single integer AAA, the minimum number of cookies Chef must bake initially.

###Constraints


1≤T≤10001 \leq T \leq 10001≤T≤1000
1≤N≤10000000001 \leq N \leq 10000000001≤N≤1000000000
2≤B≤10000000002 \leq B \leq 10000000002≤B≤1000000000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two space-separated integers N and B, where N is the minimum number of cookies Chef wants to eat and B is the number of crumbs required to make a new cookie.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum number of cookies Chef must initially bake to satisfy his hunger.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Chef needs to eat at least 3 cookies. He can make 1 new cookie from the crumbs of 2 cookies. So, he needs to bake 2 cookies initially to satisfy his hunger.
Chef needs to eat at least 11 cookies. He can make 1 new cookie from the crumbs of 2 cookies. So, he needs to bake 6 cookies initially to satisfy his hunger.

solution.approach: To find the minimum number of cookies Chef must initially bake, we can use a greedy approach. We start by calculating the minimum number of cookies needed to satisfy the hunger. If the remainder of the division of N by B-1 is not zero, we add 1 to the result to account for the extra cookie needed to make up for the remaining crumbs.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — each test case is solved in constant time
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — we only use a constant amount of space to store the input and output

code.Python3:
import math
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, B = map(int, input().split())
    print(math.ceil(N / (B - 1 + 1 / (B - 1))))
